Russia's VTB says financing of huge Ust-Luga gas complex is 'in full swing'
Sanofi Earnings Driven by New Medicine Sales, Dupixent Performance
data.description:
By Billy Gray


Sanofi said robust pharmaceutical launches and increased demand for its blockbuster Dupixent drug drove higher sales and adjusted earnings for the third quarter.

The French drugmaker on Friday reported net sales of 12.43 billion euros ($14.44 billion) for the third quarter, up 7% on year at constant currency.

Net profit was 2.80 billion euros, down 0.5% due to losses from discontinued operations. Excluding exceptional items, business net profit was 3.55 billion euros, or 2.91 euros a share.

Analysts had forecast Sanofi's sales at 12.29 billion euros and adjusted earnings per share at 2.70 euros, according to consensus estimates provided by Vara Research.

Sanofi said new medicine generated sales of 1 billion euros, up 57% from the same period last year. It said sales of the anti-inflammatory treatment Dupixent--developed jointly with Regeneron--jumped 26% to 4.2 billion euros, which allowed the company to offset a 7.8% fall in vaccine sales.

The group confirmed its full-year forecast of sales growth at a high-single-digit percentage at constant exchange rates. It said it still anticipates a strong result in business EPS, excluding share buybacks, expecting it to rise by a low-double-digit percentage at constant exchange rates.


Write to Billy Gray at william.gray@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-24-25 0300ET

data.title: Prudential Buys Back Shares
UniCredit Appoints Lindner as Advisor, Berlin Launches Investigation
data.description:
(Alliance News) - The UniCredit-Commerzbank saga has taken a new turn. As reported by la Repubblica on Friday, former German Finance Minister Christian Lindner has been hired by Teneo, the consulting firm assisting UniCredit in its bid to acquire Commerzbank.

This move has sparked suspicions of a conflict of interest in Germany, as Lindner, during his tenure as minister, did not oppose the sale of the first public shares in Commerzbank to the Italian bank.

Following the political turmoil of 2024 and the fall of the Scholz government, the new administration led by Merz has reaffirmed its opposition to the UniCredit deal. Under German law, Lindner can only take up the position with the approval of the government and the conflict of interest commission.

Meanwhile, the NGO LobbyControl and The Left party member Christian Goerke have called for the authorization to be blocked, describing Lindner's appointment as a case of "blatant conflict of interest" due to Teneo's relationships with clients such as UniCredit and Trade Republic.

By Giuseppe Fabio Ciccomascolo, Alliance News senior reporter

Comments and questions: redazione@alliancenews.com

Copyright 2025 Alliance News IS Italian Service Ltd. All rights reserved.

data.title: UK retail sales rise; NatWest lifts 2025 guidance
data.description:
(Alliance News) - NatWest Group upgraded its 2025 guidance after reporting a strong third-quarter profit rise, while GSK secured US approval for its multiple myeloma drug Blenrep.

Here is what you need to know before the London market open:

----------

MARKETS

----------

FTSE 100: called higher 0.1% at 9,585.47

GBP: higher at USD1.3328 (USD1.3323 at previous London equities close)

----------

ECONOMICS

----------

UK retail sales rise 0.5% month-on-month in September, beating expectations for a 0.2% fall and extending a four-month run of growth, according to the Office for National Statistics. Sales increase 0.9% in the three months to September compared with the previous quarter and are 1.5% higher year-on-year, though still 1.6% below pre-pandemic levels. The ONS says warmer weather boosted clothing sales, while online and mail-order demand remained strong, with non-food store sales up 0.9% and online volumes reaching their highest since February 2022. Online spending climbs 1.4% on the month and 5.6% annually, accounting for 28% of total retail sales.

----------

BROKER RATINGS

----------

Peel Hunt raises WH Smith to 'buy' (hold) - price target 800 (755) pence

----------

RBC raises Kingfisher to 'outperform' (sector perform) - price target 350 (320) pence

----------

COMPANIES - FTSE 100

----------

NatWest Group reports "strong" third-quarter results, with operating pretax profit in the three months ended September 30 up 30% year-on-year to GBP2.18 billion from GBP1.67 billion, supported by higher income and improved margins. Total income rises 15% to GBP4.33 billion from GBP3.74 billion, including net interest income of GBP3.27 billion, up 13% from a year earlier, and above Visible Alpha consensus of GBP3.20 billion, and a net interest margin of 2.37%, compared with 2.18% a year prior. Total income excluding notable items grows 10% to GBP4.17 billion. Impairment losses narrow to GBP153 million from GBP245 million, while litigation and conduct costs drop sharply to GBP12 million from GBP41 million. NatWest's return on tangible equity climbs to 22.3% from 18.3% a year earlier, and its common equity tier 1 ratio strengthens to 14.2% from 13.6%. Assets under management and administration rise 8.1% in the quarter to GBP56.0 billion, driven by strong client net inflows. The bank upgrades its 2025 guidance, now expecting income excluding notable items of around GBP16.3 billion, up from GBP16 billion previously, and a return on tangible equity of above 18%. Chief Executive Paul Thwaite says performance remains strong, underpinned by "healthy levels of customer activity" and ongoing simplification to support growth and efficiency.

----------

GSK secures US Food & Drug Administration approval for Blenrep, belantamab mafodotin-blmf, in combination with bortezomib and dexamethasone to treat adults with relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma after at least two prior lines of therapy. The decision is based on phase III Dreamm-7 trial data, which showed a 51% reduction in the risk of death and a tripling of median progression-free survival to 31.3 months versus 10.4 months for a daratumumab-based regimen. Chief Scientific Officer Tony Wood says the approval marks "another significant milestone," offering the only anti-BCMA therapy available in community settings where 70% of patients receive care. GSK introduces a streamlined risk management programme to ease patient access and is advancing additional trials to evaluate Blenrep in earlier treatment lines, with long-term survival data expected in 2028.

----------

COMPANIES - FTSE 250

----------

JTC extends its 'put up or shut up' deadline for prospective bidders Permira and Warburg Pincus to 1700 GMT on November 7, with the UK Takeover Panel's consent under Rule 2.6(c). The trust and corporate services group says talks with both parties are ongoing following prior preliminary, non-binding approaches to acquire the entire share capital. JTC reiterates there is no certainty a firm offer will be made.

----------

VinaCapital Vietnam Opportunity Fund reports a disappointing performance for the financial year ended June 30, with a net asset value total return of negative 2.2% in USD, compared with a positive 7.8% the year before. NAV per share falls 4.0% to USD7.13 from USD7.43, while the share price declines 7.8% to USD5.77. The fund posts a loss per share of 33 US cents, compared with earnings of 47 cents in the prior year, though it maintains its annual dividend at 14.5 US cents per share. Manager VinaCapital Investment Management says "Although the results for the year might be a disappointment, performance in the first three months of the current financial year has shown a substantial improvement with the NAV per share up by 12.4% and the share price improving by 13.3%, both in USD terms."

----------

OTHER COMPANIES

----------

Pan African Resources expects its shares to start trading on the main board of the London Stock Exchange from Friday. The Rosebank-headquartered gold producer also said on Friday it expects shares to be admitted to the equity shares category of the official list maintained by the UK Financial Conduct Authority from Friday. The company first announced its plan to move its listing to the main board of the LSE early last month. Back then, it indicated that the listing transfer does not intend to raise any funds or offer any new securities. Pan African Resources ends its presence on the AIM Market of the LSE, where is has been listed since 2007. The junior gold producer has said it believes that the proposed move to the Main Market could enhance its corporate profile and broaden its access to a wider pool of UK and global investors, supporting its next phase of growth.

----------

Revel Collective launches a strategic review and formal sale process after what it calls a "continued period of external challenges" affecting trading. The bar and pub operator, which owns the Revolution, Revolucion de Cuba and Peach Pubs brands, says first-quarter group revenue fell 7.4% year-on-year on a like-for-like basis to GBP26.3 million, with bar sales down 11%. Despite cost-cutting measures, the group says changes in the UK autumn budget – including higher national insurance, minimum wage and spirits duty – have cost over GBP4 million annually. Revel expects a significant profit contribution from the festive trading period but warns it will need additional funding early in 2026 to stay within banking limits. The company, advised by Cavendish and FTI Consulting, says it is considering a range of options including a sale of the business or its brands, though it is not currently in talks with any potential offeror.

----------

By Eva Castanedo, Alliance News reporter

Comments and questions to newsroom@alliancenews.com

Copyright 2025 Alliance News Ltd. All Rights Reserved.

NatWest Lifts Guidance Again After Strong Customer Activity Boosts Quarter
data.description:
By Elena Vardon


NatWest Group raised its revenue and profitability guidance for the second time this year after strong customer activity helped its third-quarter results top market expectations.

The U.K. high-street bank said operating pretax profit jumped 30% on year to 2.18 billion pounds ($2.91 billion) for the three months ended Sept. 30. Analysts had expected 1.83 billion pounds, according to a company-compiled consensus.

The lender reported a 16% increase in total income to 4.33 billion pounds, beating a 4.10 billion-pound forecast.

NatWest, which is known for issuing conservative financial targets, said it now expects total income excluding notable items for 2025 to come in around 16.3 billion pounds as loan growth and structural hedges continue to mitigate the impact of interest-rate cuts by the Bank of England. This compares with its previous guidance of income greater than 16.0 billion pounds, and current analysts' expectations of 16.22 billion pounds.

The London-listed group also raised its annual return on tangible equity forecast--a key measure of profitability--to more than 18.0%, after reporting a better-than-expected third-quarter return of 22.3%. Previous guidance was for a return greater than 16.5%, while consensus pencils in 17.3% for the year.


Write to Elena Vardon at elena.vardon@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-24-25 0248ET

Ford exceeds quarterly expectations but lowers its 2025 forecast after fire at plant
data.description:
Ford Motor reported better-than-expected quarterly results but lowered its outlook for 2025 following a fire at a plant owned by its aluminum supplier Novelis in New York. The incident, which is affecting pickup truck and SUV production, is expected to cost between $1.5bn and $2bn, although the automaker expects a partial recovery as early as next year. Ford forecasts adjusted operating profit (EBIT) of between $6bn and $6.5bn for 2025, down from a previous range of $6.5bn to $7.5bn, with adjusted cash flow of between $2bn and $3bn. In extended trading, the stock fell about 1%.

CEO Jim Farley said production of the affected models would be gradually increased as soon as the aluminum supply stabilized. Ford announced the addition of 1,000 jobs at its Michigan and Kentucky plants in 2026 to offset the estimated loss of 50,000 vehicles. According to management, the damaged part of the Novelis site is expected to restart by the end of the year. CFO Sherry House said that without this incident, Ford would have raised its EBIT forecast to over  $8bn.

However, Q3 results confirm a solid performance. Adjusted EPS was 45 cents, compared with 36 cents expected, for total revenue of $50.5bn, up 9% y-o-y. Net income reached $2.4bn, nearly triple last year's figure. Ford also benefited from the extension of a customs countermeasure decided by Washington, which should halve the impact of taxes on its vehicles produced in the US. The group is also maintaining its investments at around $9bn and confirming its target of $1bn in savings this year as part of its Ford+ plan.

Futures up, focus on Eni after quarterly results
data.description:
(Alliance News) - The Italian stock market is expected to rise at the start of a day that sees the publication of preliminary PMIs for the main Eurozone economies as the focus of the macro calendar.

All eyes are on Eni, which announced before the market opened that it had recorded an adjusted net profit of EUR1.25 billion in the third quarter, down 2% from EUR1.27 billion in the same period in 2024.

Adjusted pro-forma EBIT was EUR 3.00 billion, down 12% from EUR 3.40 billion a year earlier. Adjusted pre-tax profit, on the other hand, fell 14% year-on-year in the third quarter, to EUR 2.27 billion from EUR 2.66 billion.

Hydrocarbon production increased by 6% year-on-year, reaching 1.76 million barrels per day from 1.66 million, thanks to the start-up of new projects and solid operational performance.

In the nine months, however, net profit rose to EUR2.52 billion from EUR2.39 billion in the nine months of 2024, with the adjusted figure down 13% to EUR3.79 billion from EUR4.37 billion. Adjusted pre-tax profit for the nine months fell to EUR7.22 billion from EUR9.20 billion, and adjusted EBIT fell 19% to EUR9.36 billion from EUR11.62 billion.

Eni also increased its buyback program for this year to EUR1.8 billion, up EUR300 million from its previous forecast, and confirmed its annual dividend at EUR1.05 per share, with the second tranche of EUR0.26 payable on November 26, as announced yesterday.

The FTSE Mib is up 172.5 points or 0.4% after closing up 0.4% at 42,381.93 on Thursday.

The FTSE 100 in London is up 7.1 points or 0.1%, the CAC 40 in Paris is expected to rise 35.6 points or 0.4%, and the DAX 40 in Frankfurt is expected to rise 84.0 points or 0.4%.

Last night in Milan, the Mid-Cap rose 0.4% to 57,588.25, the Small-Cap closed up 0.3% at 36,861.95, and the Italy Growth improved 0.3% to 8,690.96.

Yesterday was a good day for Eni and A2A on the Milan Stock Exchange, with gains of 3.3% to EUR15.618 per share and 3.0% to EUR2.437 per share, respectively. Eni carried out a buyback of EUR50.0 million.

The day of quarterly results did not smile on STMicroelectronics, which fell 14% after slowing growth and posting net income of USD237 million, or USD0.26 per diluted share, compared to USD381 million in the same period in 2024.

On a non-US GAAP basis, net income was USD267 million, equivalent to USD0.29 per diluted share. Gross profit was USD1.06 billion, down 14% year-on-year.

Saipem, on the other hand, rose 1.3% after presenting its quarterly results: the company reported net income of EUR221 million, up from EUR206 million in the same period of 2024. In the third quarter alone, net income was EUR81 million, down from EUR88 million in the corresponding quarter of the previous year.

Leonardo, up 1.7%, together with Airbus SE and Thales, announced the signing of a memorandum of understanding for the creation of a new European space company, which is expected to be operational in 2027 after obtaining regulatory approvals.

Stellantis reported a 12% increase in car sales in Europe in September, confirming its strong performance in the hybrid segment. To address staffing shortages, the company has begun recruiting workers from Nepal, India, Bangladesh, and Bhutan. The stock fell 1.3%.

UniCredit, up 1.3%, launched the second and final tranche of its "Residuo SBB 2024" share buyback program, which provides for a maximum investment of EUR1.77 billion and the purchase of no more than 82.5 million ordinary shares, equal to approximately 5% of the share capital. On Thursday, Barclays raised its target price on UniCredit to EUR71.60 from EUR69.20, confirming its 'overweight' recommendation on the stock.

In the mid-cap segment, Cembre was the best performer, up 6.6% to EUR63.30 per share.

Tensions continue between Tether, led by Giancarlo Devasini and Paolo Ardoino, and John Elkann's Exor over Juventus. Ahead of the November 7 shareholders' meeting, the Bianconeri board recommended that shareholders vote against all proposals presented by Tether, which holds 11.5% of the capital: the stock still gained 2.1%.

MAIRE, down 0.3%, announced on Thursday through its subsidiary NextChem the launch of Next-N, which has been awarded a contract worth EUR70 million by Newcleo. Among other things, the company closed the first nine months of 2025 with a net profit of EUR 204.8 million, up 42% from EUR 144.5 million in the same period of 2024.

Ferretti, which lost 0.9%, closed the first nine months of 2025 with a net profit of EUR 61 million, down slightly from EUR 62.2 million in the same period of 2024. Net revenues rose to EUR 887.2 million from EUR 865.3 million in the first nine months of 2024, marking an increase of 2.5%.

Alerion Clean Power, which closed up 0.8%, began preparations for the issuance of a senior unsecured, non-convertible, and unsubordinated bond with a fixed value of EUR100 million.

On the Small Cap market, EEMS, which approved the change of the company's name to Ops Italia, with the aim of reflecting the new identity and industrial strategy outlined in the 2025-2028 plan, closed down 1.8%.

Piquadro recorded a slight increase in turnover in the first half of the year ending September 30: the stock was down 2.4%.

Among SMEs, Gismondi 1754, which was not traded, approved its consolidated operating results for the third quarter of 2025. During the period, the company recorded total sales of EUR 2.1 million, down 21% from EUR 2.6 million in the same quarter of 2024.

The benefit corporation ICOP, down 3.3%, announced that Consob had approved the offer document relating to the total takeover bid for Palingeo, down 1.3%. The offer, promoted by ICOP, concerns a maximum of approximately 2.7 million ordinary shares of Palingeo, equal to the entire capital involved in the transaction.

Xenon AIFM, manager of the Xenon Private Equity VIII SCA SICAV-RAIF fund, announced two voluntary takeover bids for Eles Semiconductor Equipment, which gained 15%.

Renovalo - down 3.0% - announced on Thursday that the last exercise period for the 2022-2025 warrants will open on November 3 and run until November 17, during which time warrant holders will be able to subscribe to one new Renovalo ordinary share for every four warrants exercised, at a price of EUR10.08 per share.

Good purchases for 12% on Rino Petino and 13% on Braga Moro.

In Asia, the Nikkei is up 1.4% to 49,326.40, the Shanghai Composite is up 0.6% to 3,946.67, and the Hang Seng is up 0.5% to 26,107.82.

In New York, at Thursday's close, the Dow Jones rose 0.3% to 46,734.61, the Nasdaq gained 0.9% to 22,941.80, and the S&P 500 rose 0.6% to 6,738.44.

On the currency front, the euro is trading at USD1.1606 from USD1.1608 at Thursday's stock market close, while the pound is trading at USD1.3325 from USD1.3321 last night.

Among commodities, Brent crude traded at USD 65.79 per barrel, up from USD 65.67 per barrel at yesterday evening's close, while gold was worth USD 4,089.58 per ounce, down from USD 4,146.60 per ounce yesterday evening.

On Friday's economic calendar, the French consumer confidence index is expected at 0845 CEST and Spanish unemployment data is expected at 0900 CEST.

Between 0915 and 1030 CEST, preliminary PMIs for France, Germany, the Eurozone, and the UK will be released.

The US construction situation will be assessed at 1430 CEST along with consumer prices, while the focus on Michigan - inflation and sentiment - comes at 1600 CEST.

On the Italian stock exchange, results are expected from Allcore, EPH, and Next RE.

By Giuseppe Fabio Ciccomascolo, Alliance News senior reporter

Comments and questions to redazione@alliancenews.com

Copyright 2025 Alliance News IS Italian Service Ltd. All rights reserved.

data.title: Origin Energy Shares Decline After It Reportedly Informed Over 700 Customers of Potential Data Theft
Strategic capital raises stake in Keihanshin Building Co Ltd to 8.28% from 7.14%, according to filing
UBS Names New Chief Operating Officer in Executive Team Reshuffle -- Update
data.description:
By Adria Calatayud


UBS Group named Beatriz Martin, head of noncore assets, new chief operating officer in a reshuffle of its executive team as the bank nominated compliance chief Markus Ronner as vice chairman of its board.

The Swiss bank said Friday that Lukas Gaehwiler, vice chairman of its board since 2022, wouldn't stand for re-election at next year's annual general meeting as he decided to retire.

UBS proposed Ronner, currently the group's chief compliance and governance officer, to succeed Gaehwiler as board vice chair. It also appointed Michelle Bereaux, chief integration officer, as head compliance and operational risk control, and transferred responsibility for governmental and regulatory affairs under the remit of Todd Tuckner, its finance chief.

The executive shake-up puts Martin in charge of finalizing the integration of Credit Suisse as COO. She will keep her current responsibilities as sustainability lead and head of the group's noncore and legacy business--which houses assets formerly owned by Credit Suisse it wants to exit--as well as president for the Europe, Middle East and Africa region and chief executive for the U.K., UBS said.

Bereaux, who was named chief group integration officer in 2023 and led the completion of client migrations of Switzerland and part of the work in the bank's home country, will relinquish her responsibilities to assume her new role, UBS said.

The bank said Gaehwiler, who is retiring after a 45-year career in the financial sector, played a key role in the integration of Credit Suisse, having served as its final chair.

"With these changes, UBS is ideally positioned to successfully complete the integration, focus on growth opportunities and continue to deliver in the best interest of all our key stakeholders," UBS Chief Executive Sergio Ermotti said.

UBS said it remains on track to complete the Credit Suisse integration by the end of 2026.


Write to Adria Calatayud at adria.calatayud@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-24-25 0239ET

data.title: ASEAN to host Trump at summit as US and China seek to avert trade war escalation
Vincent Rouget will take over as head of URW in early 2026
data.description:
Unibail-Rodamco-Westfield (URW) announces that Vincent Rouget, currently Chief Strategy and Investment Officer, who serves as Chief Operating Officer for Europe, has been appointed Chairman of the Management Board effective January 1, 2026.

This decision reflects the real estate group's outlook and the positive trajectory set out in the 2025-28 roadmap established by Jean-Marie Tritant, Chairman of the Management Board since 2021, who will oversee the transition until the end of the year.

In addition, URW announces the acquisition of a 25% stake in St James Quarter, a mixed-use destination in Edinburgh combining 110 retail, leisure and dining units across 80,300 sq m, with 152 high-end apartments.

As part of this new joint venture partnership with majority shareholder APG, which will retain a 75% stake, URW will manage the asset, which will be renamed Westfield in 2026.

data.title: Eni Increases Buyback, Cost-Savings Target
data.description:
By Adam Whittaker


Eni increased its full-year buyback commitment after raising its cost-savings target, as third-quarter earnings rose with higher production offsetting weaker oil prices.

The Italian oil-and-gas major said Friday that it will now buy back 1.8 billion euros ($2.09 billion) of shares over the full year, a 300 million euro increase on its previous commitment as it benefits from a higher free cash flow and cash-saving initiatives.

Eni also increased its full-year cash flow before working capital adjustments guidance to 12 billion euros from 11.5 billion euros, and said that it will target around 4 billion euros in costs savings, up from a previous target of 3 billion euros.

The guidance upgrades came as Eni's earnings rose and beat expectations. Net profit rose to 803 million euros from 543 million euros in the prior quarter. Adjusted net profit increased to 1.25 billion euros, rising from 1.13 billion euros and beating the 1.02 billion euros analysts had expected according to a company-compiled consensus.

The company said crude oil prices declined 14% over the quarter but this was partially offset by a rise in production to 1.76 million barrels of oil equivalent a day. Eni increased its oil-and-gas production guidance to between 1.71 million and 1.72 million barrels of oil equivalent a day.


Write to Adam Whittaker at adam.whittaker@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-24-25 0237ET

data.title: Stallion India to Start Construction at New Manufacturing Unit in Bhilwara, India; Shares Down 5%
Sanofi increases EPS by 13% at CGU in Q3
data.description:
Sanofi has reported Q3 2025 operating EPS of €2.91, up 7% (+13.2% at constant exchange rates or CCE), "supported by disciplined cost management, a focus on R&D and operational efficiency."

Its sales increased by 2.3% to €12.43bn (+7% at CGU). Sales of new pharmaceutical launches climbed 57.1% to €1bn, and sales of Dupixent rose 26.2% to €4.2bn, but vaccine sales declined 7.8% to €3.4bn.

As expected, sales of influenza vaccines declined due to increased price competition and lower vaccination rates, management said.

Sanofi confirms that it expects a strong rebound in EPS from its activities in 2025, with growth at the low end of the double-digit range (excluding share buybacks), and anticipates high single-digit growth in sales at constant currency.

Hexagon Swings to Net Loss in Q3
Hexagon's adjusted operating profit higher than expected (update)
data.description:
(addition in full news item)

Measurement technology company Hexagon reports sales in line with expectations for the third quarter.
Adjusted operating profit was better than expected.

"Organic growth amounted to 4 percent, driven mainly by another strong quarter in Autonomous Solutions and good demand in segments such as aerospace and defense, machine control, and electronics. The operating margin improved sequentially but remains below our target. Cash generation was seasonally strong. The potential spin-off of Octave is proceeding according to plan and is expected to be completed during the first half of
2026," says CEO Anders Svensson.

Sales rose 0.3 percent to EUR 1,303.8 million (1,299.8). The outcome can be compared with Bloomberg's analyst consensus of 1,317.

Adjusted operating profit amounted to EUR 349.3 million (376.6), compared with an expected EUR 336 million, with an adjusted operating margin of 26.8 percent (29.0).

Operating profit amounted to EUR -12.9 million (333.4). Adjustments are included at EUR -362.2 million (-42.2).

Operating cash flow before non-recurring items increased to EUR 177.8 million (165.9).

Cash generation amounted to 77 percent (70).

Recurring revenue increased organically by 6 percent and amounted to EUR 564.2 million (564.9).

"Signals from our markets point to an unchanged climate in the fourth quarter of 2025," Anders Svensson continues.

Hexagon announced in its Q2 report that a cost-saving program had been initiated.

"In our review of the cost base, we identified potential savings of EUR 110 million on an annual basis, with a one-time cost of EUR 113 million. These savings, which cover both Hexagon and Octave, will gradually begin to positively impact the cost base from the fourth quarter of 2025 and reach full effect towards the end of 2026," says Svensson.

He adds.

"We have also identified investments in research and development and related inventories totaling €186 million that no longer correspond to customer or market needs. We have therefore decided to write down these items."
